channel.title: Pharma AI & Data Watch
channel.description: Curated AI, pharma, and regulatory science intelligence — updated weekly.

item.title: Rnabpflow: base pair-augmented se(3) flow matching for conditional rna 3d structure generation
item.description: RNAbpFlow generates all-atom RNA conformational ensembles for single-chain RNA monomers without the explicit or implicit use of evolutionary information or homologous structural templates.

item.title: AI will drive africa’s sustainable development, conference declares
item.description: Artificial Intelligence (AI) has been identified as a critical catalyst for accelerating innovation, strengthening governance, improving institutional resilience and advancing sustainable development across Africa and the Global South, as experts at an intern…

item.title: Hpe discover 2026: powering siemens healthineers’ life-saving vision
item.description: Siemens Healthineers’ head of clinical marketing discusses the technology behind the firm’s advances in medical imaging

item.title: Retraction note: nsd2 targeting reverses plasticity and drug resistance in prostate cancer
item.description: Nature - Retraction Note: NSD2 targeting reverses plasticity and drug resistance in prostate cancer

item.title: In an era of AI darwinism, roi now represents return on intelligence
item.description: You can’t cut your way to growth. You can’t automate your way to innovation. AI is forcing every organization to reveal what it really believes about people. Do leaders see AI as a way to shrink the company into a more efficient version of yesterday? Or do th…

item.title: Vibe coding platform base44 launches own model as AI startups seek defensibility | techcrunch
item.description: Wix-owned vibe coding platform Base44 has started rolling out its own AI model — with hopes that it will eventually outperform frontier models.

item.title: Ophthalmic self-medication among adult ophthalmic patients attending a tertiary eye care centre in Northwest Ethiopia: a mixed-methods study assessing prevalence, associated factors and lived experience
item.description: This mixed-methods study evaluated the prevalence of self-medication practices for eye conditions among adult patients at a tertiary eye care centre in Northwest Ethiopia, identifying associated risk factors and documenting patients' firsthand experiences with self-treatment. The research combined q

item.title: Safety evaluation of CD3×CD20 bispecific antibodies: a pharmacovigilance study using FDA adverse event reporting system
item.description: This pharmacovigilance study analyzed adverse event reports from the FDA system to evaluate the safety profile of CD3×CD20 bispecific antibodies, a class of immunotherapeutic agents designed to engage both T cells and B cells in cancer treatment. The research systematically identified and characteri

item.title: Collaborative development of a digital intervention to support opioid tapering after surgery in primary care: an experience-based co-design study with patients and clinicians
item.description: Researchers used experience-based co-design methodology involving patients and clinicians to develop a digital intervention aimed at supporting opioid tapering in primary care following surgical procedures. The collaborative approach integrated patient and healthcare provider perspectives to create

item.title: FDA Approves New Treatment That Uses Donor Immune Cells to Prevent Serious Complications in Blood Cancer Patients
item.description: The U.S. Food and Drug Administration today approved TREGZI, the first regulatory T (Treg) cell-based immunotherapy for improving chronic graft-versus-host disease (GVHD)-free survival in adult patients with blood cancers undergoing allogeneic hematopoietic stem cell transplantation (allo-HSCT).

item.title: STAT+: Anthropic releases Claude Science, a product aimed at researchers, the pharma industry
item.description: Anthropic released Claude Science, an application that optimizes its large language model for scientists and, especially, those doing research at pharma companies.

item.title: STAT+: Investors double down on Bain-backed startup
item.description: FDA's top gene therapy regulator steps down, Trump's plan to lower GLP-1 prices, and more biotech news

item.title: The DeepMind trio who built a poker AI are now making money for quant hedge funds
item.description: EquiLibre Technologies, a Prague-based AI lab founded by three ex-DeepMind researchers, is now valued at more than $500 million.

item.title: Anthropic launches Claude Sonnet 5 as a cheaper way to run agents
item.description: Anthropic’s Claude Sonnet 5 brings stronger agentic capabilities, lower pricing, and improved safety, positioning the model as a cheaper alternative to Opus, GPT-5.5, and Gemini Pro.

item.title: Acti puts AI agents directly into your smartphone keyboard
item.description: Acti is betting the smartphone keyboard is the next home for AI assistants. The startup's new keyboard for iOS and Android works across apps and lets users create custom AI-powered shortcuts using natural language.

item.title: Anthropic’s Claude Science bets on workflow, not a new model, to win over scientists
item.description: Anthropic's Claude Science is a workbench that gives scientists one environment to do computational research, saving them from the need to bounce between databases, pipelines, and tools.

item.title: Amazon launches new $1 billion FDE org, following OpenAI and Anthropic
item.description: Engineers on the new team will embed within companies to deploy purpose-built agents, focusing on fast deployments and customer self-sufficiency.

item.title: Claude Science is Anthropic’s newest flagship product
item.description: At an event for pharmaceutical executives, biotech founders, and researchers on Tuesday, Anthropic announced Claude Science, a major new product intended to support scientific research in the same way that Claude Code supports software engineering. Like Claude Code, Claude Science can autonomously c

item.title: The Download: AI “coworkers” and stratospheric internet
item.description: This is today’s edition of The Download, our weekday newsletter that provides a daily dose of what’s going on in the world of technology. AI agents are not your “coworkers” Imagine coming in to work to learn that a new underling will report to you. The worker is not a person but an AI to

item.title: Agriculture is ready for AI, but its data isn’t
item.description: Artificial intelligence is transforming what is possible in agriculture, but industry leaders should be wary of investing in AI without first laying the groundwork.  The use cases are promising, especially for an industry navigating volatile fertilizer costs, unpredictable weather, and margins

item.title: Building tech in the world’s secret R&D hub
item.description: Apple. Anthropic. Disney Research. Google. Meta. Microsoft. NVIDIA. OpenAI. Few places outside Silicon Valley can claim R&D hubs from all of these companies. Fewer still are concentrated in a city of just over 400,000 people—roughly half the size of San Francisco. Over the past two decades, howe

item.title: STAT+: Sword Health contracted to provide AI-supported physical therapy for an entire country
item.description: Portugal's National Health Service signed a deal with digital health company Sword for its AI-assisted virtual physical therapy care.

item.title: STAT+: AI scientist company Edison Scientific tapped by team behind Metsera to create new biotechs
item.description: Edison Scientific and investment firm Population Health Partners are teaming up to leverage AI agents in drug discovery and development.

item.title: Voters of both parties want tighter AI regulation, poll finds
item.description: A new survey from the AI Policy Institute finds bipartisan support for legislation that targets powerful AI systems.

item.title: Use of AI for gxp inspection responses: setting standards without stifling innovation
item.description: This blog specifically relates to submissions to the MHRA Compliance Teams following GxP inspections. The MHRA has identified examples of information relating to inspection activity submitted to compliance teams which have been generated by Artificial Intelli…

item.title: Beyond vivatech: south korea’s strategy to anchor its startups in the european ecosystem
item.description: In the global competition to attract talent, capital, and technology, major innovation events now play a role that extends far beyond simple business networking. They have become spaces where strategic alliances take shape, international ambitions are tested,…

item.title: Twit 1090: flock of sqls - apple & microsoft grapple with soaring hardware prices
item.description: Tech giants and chipmakers are facing off as AI-fueled memory shortages trigger sweeping price hikes on everything from Macs to game consoles. Hear why global supply chain standoffs, long-term contracts, and old-school market forces are quietly reshaping your…

item.title: Imaging the hallmarks of cancer
item.description: In this Review, Grimm et al. describe the range of non-invasive diagnostic imaging approaches to monitor the various hallmarks of cancer and how their implementation is shaping clinical practice.

item.title: Ai-driven patient detection surfaces approximately 1,200 likely-undiagnosed gep-net patients in UK primary care
item.description: ~1,200 likely-undiagnosed GEP-NET patients detected in 24M UK primary care records, flagged patients averaging 5–7 years younger than those already diagnosed.

item.title: Telling internet platforms where to stick public service media will serve nobody. turn it on its head
item.description: Show not tell makes a far finer script

item.title: Current Status and Construction Requirements of Drug Clinical Trial Institutions in Sichuan Province in China
item.description: This study examines the current infrastructure and development needs of drug clinical trial institutions across Sichuan Province in China. The research, authored by investigators from West China Second University Hospital and affiliated research laboratories, provides an assessment of institutional

item.title: EGFR-dependent delivery of phosphorylated IGFBP3 by cancer stem cells drives chemotherapy resistance in colorectal cancer
item.description: Cancer stem cells in colorectal cancer secrete phosphorylated IGFBP3 protein through an EGFR-dependent mechanism, which promotes chemotherapy resistance in tumor cells. This finding identifies a novel pathway by which cancer stem cells communicate with bulk tumor cells to reduce drug sensitivity. Th

item.title: FDA Selects Seven Participants for PreCheck Pilot Program to Advance U.S. Drug Manufacturing
item.description: FDA announces the selection of seven companies to participate in the FDA PreCheck Pilot Program, a new initiative designed to boost American global competitiveness, strengthen domestic drug manufacturing, and improve the resilience of the U.S. drug supply chain.

item.title: Anthropic and Gov. Newsom forge deal allowing California government to use Claude at half price
item.description: As Anthropic forges a closer relationship with the state of California, the federal government has made an enemy out of the OpenAI rival.

item.title: AI agents are not your “coworkers”
item.description: This story originally appeared in The Algorithm, our weekly newsletter on AI. To get stories like this in your inbox first, sign up here. Imagine coming in to work to learn that a new underling will report to you. The worker is not a person but an AI tool—one that your company nonetheless calls Alex

item.title: Agent confidence on the technical frontier
item.description: Enterprise investment in AI is booming. Gartner is calling 2026 an “inflection year” for organizations to align their AI projects with strategic business objectives. As the pressure to prove ROI mounts, executives and technology leaders are looking to agentic AI to drive the measurable financial out

item.title: Import AI 463: Self-improving robots; a 10k Chinese GPU cluster; and an elegiac essay for the human era
item.description: Welcome to Import AI, a newsletter about AI research. Import AI runs on arXiv, cappuccinos, and feedback from readers. If you’d like to support this, please subscribe. Subscribe now NVIDIA sets up a crude self-improvement loop for real world robotics:…What if you could take the best ideas from AI ag

item.title: Don’t be afraid of self-improving AI, says a16z-backed startup mirendil
item.description: Some call it a dangerous path to runaway AI, others call it vibe research.

item.title: AI scientist company edison scientific tapped by team behind metsera to create new biotechs
item.description: The first time a top-10 drugmaker called AI scientist company FutureHouse, offering $30 million to use the nonprofit’s artificial intelligence agents for drug discovery, CEO Sam Rodriques thought there was a misunderstanding; the offer couldn’t be real, he sa…

item.title: Ghf-acl: a novel contrastive learning framework with multi-order graph structures for herb-disease association prediction
item.description: Author summary Traditional Chinese Medicine has been used for centuries, but understanding how it works at a scientific level is still difficult. One reason is that herbs are naturally complex. Instead of relying on a single active substance, most herbs conta…

item.title: Daily briefing: editing the epigenome with crispr to treat disease
item.description: CRISPR’s next act, the new ‘world’s fastest computer’ and how to triumph in the metaphorical penalty shootout of life.

item.title: Google makes gemini’s personalized image generation free for all US users
item.description: Google is making Gemini’s personalized AI image generation free for all eligible users in the United States, removing a paywall that had restricted the feature to Plus, Pro, and Ultra subscribers since its launch in April. The expansion, announced on Sunday, …

item.title: Cboe canada welcomes listing of guideai health corp.
item.description: AI-Driven Cardiovascular Diagnostics Company Brings Early Detection Technology to Capital Markets TORONTO — Cboe Canada Inc. (“Cboe Canada”) today announced the listing of GuideAI Health Corp. (“GuideAI”), an artificial intelligence healthcare company focused…

item.title: Build an agentic AI healthcare claims pipeline with amazon bedrock and aws healthlake
item.description: In this post, we show you how to build an automated claims processing pipeline using two key Amazon Bedrock capabilities: Amazon Bedrock Data Automation for intelligent document extraction from healthcare claim forms, and Amazon Bedrock AgentCore for hosting …

item.title: Academic transcription is core infrastructure in canada’s universities, but risks remain
item.description: The projected expansion of the academic transcription market reflects a broader transformation in higher education, one that is particularly evident in Canada. Yet the rise of AI-driven services introduces new complexities around accuracy, privacy, and govern…

item.title: Two new studies confirm the 2025–2026 covid booster works, including a 59% reduction in doctor visit risk
item.description: Two June 2026 studies confirm the 2025–26 COVID vaccine is working. One found 59% reduced risk of needing a doctor visit. Here's what both studies show and who they apply to.

item.title: Multi-tenant LLM analytics with row-level security: how we built a secure agent on aws
item.description: In this post, we show you how PAR built a production-ready multi-tenant LLM analytics system that enforces row-level security through a three-layer architecture: cryptographic request signing with AWS SigV4, semantic validation on Amazon Bedrock, and programm…

item.title: Epidemiology of invasive pneumococcal disease in southwest sweden during the first eleven years after the introduction of general childhood pneumococcal vaccination
item.description: Background Invasive pneumococcal disease (IPD) still causes significant morbidity and mortality. In this study, we describe incidence, risk factors, manifestations, and outcome of IPD in Southwest Sweden during the first eleven years after the introduction of…

item.title: Divergence between facial expressions and self-reported emotions: sex differences in responses to video-based emotional stimuli
item.description: Emotional responses involve multiple components, including subjective experience and behavioral expression, which do not always align. Moreover, sex differences in emotional processing appear to vary across emotion types and response modalities. This study in…

item.title: Security researchers tricked llms into giving them cocaine recipes by abusing role models for prompt injection
item.description: If you want a picture of the future of LLM security, imagine Whac-a-Mole meets Groundhog Day
